claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 3 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 2 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 2 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 3 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for UK Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin the Child Benefit eligibility assessment.

**First, I need to know about the child you're claiming for:**

How old is the child (or children) you want to claim Child Benefit for?

- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

Please let me know the age(s) of the child(ren) you're enquiring about, and their name(s) so I can keep track if there's more than one.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, yes I'm looking to claim Child Benefit. I've got three kids actually - Alex who's 3, Blake who's 8, and Charlie who's 2. So they're all under 16.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you for providing those details! With Alex (3), Blake (8), and Charlie (2) all being under 16, they automatically meet the age requirement for Child Benefit.

Let me continue with the next check for your children.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Are all three children (Alex, Blake, and Charlie) working less than 24 hours per week, or not in paid work at all?

Given their ages (3, 8, and 2), I assume none of them are in paid employment - is that correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right, none of them work. They're all just little ones at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, that makes sense! Since Alex, Blake, and Charlie are all little ones not in paid work, they meet this requirement.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Are all three children **not** receiving any benefits in their own right, such as Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit?

Again, given their young ages (3, 8, and 2), I'd expect this doesn't apply to them - can you confirm none of them are claiming these benefits in their own name?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of them get any benefits in their own name. They're far too young for that sort of thing.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Exactly, that's what I thought! Let's continue with the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Are any of your children (Alex, Blake, or Charlie) currently in an apprenticeship?

Given their ages (3, 8, and 2), I'm confident they're not in any apprenticeship programmes - is that correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, of course not - they're all just little kids. No apprenticeships here!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Absolutely! With children that young, apprenticeships are definitely not on the radar yet. Let's move on to check your situation as the claimant.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Now I need to check your residency status.**

Do you meet any of the following UK residency requirements? You only need to meet **one** of these:

1. **Normally live in the UK** - You usually live in the UK
2. **Settled status** - You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. **Pre-settled status and working** - You have pre-settled status and are working/earning above the National Insurance threshold
4. **Pre-settled status and seeking work** - You have pre-settled status and are actively looking for work
5. **Pre-settled status with sufficient resources** - You have pre-settled status and can financially support yourself and your family
6. **Pre-settled status and studying** - You have pre-settled status and are a student with sufficient resources
7. **Pre-settled status as a family member** - You have pre-settled status as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national
8. **Crown servant abroad** - You're living abroad as a Crown servant

Which of these applies to you? For most people, the first option (normally living in the UK) is the relevant one.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - we're in Manchester. I've lived here all my life.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, living in Manchester all your life means you clearly meet the UK residency requirement. Let's continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next, I need to confirm you're responsible for the children.**

Do you meet any of these criteria for Alex, Blake, and Charlie? You only need to meet **one**:

1. **Living with the children** - They live with you
2. **Contributing to their upkeep** - You pay at least the Child Benefit amount (or equivalent) towards their care (food, clothes, pocket money, housing, etc.)
3. **Paying maintenance** - You make maintenance payments under a court order or agreement

You mentioned they're "at home with me" - does that mean all three children live with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - all three of them live at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Since all three children live with you, you clearly meet the responsibility requirement. Let's continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Important question about other claimants:**

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, Blake, or Charlie? For example, the children's other parent or another family member?

- **No one else is claiming** for any of them
- **Someone else was claiming, but we've agreed I should claim instead**
- **There's a dispute** about who should claim

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, no one else is claiming for them. It's just me looking after them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That's clear, thank you. Let's move on to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**About where the children are living:**

Are Alex, Blake, and Charlie all living at home with you in a normal arrangement? Or is any of them currently:

- **In hospital** for an extended stay
- **In local authority care**
- **In a foster care arrangement**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, they're all just living at home with me in a normal arrangement. Nothing like that applies to any of them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, all three children are living at home in a normal arrangement. Let's continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Final question:**

Is the local council **not** paying towards the accommodation or maintenance for any of your children (Alex, Blake, or Charlie)?

In other words, is the council free from contributing to housing or supporting your children financially?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right - the council isn't paying anything towards them. I'm supporting them myself.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent news! That was the final check, and you've met all the eligibility requirements.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## 🎉 Great News - You're Eligible for Child Benefit!

You are **eligible** to claim Child Benefit for all three of your children:

| Child | Age | Eligible? |
|-------|-----|-----------|
| **Alex** | 3 | ✅ Yes |
| **Blake** | 8 | ✅ Yes |
| **Charlie** | 2 | ✅ Yes |

### Why You're Eligible
You meet all the requirements:
- ✅ All children are under 16
- ✅ None are in paid work
- ✅ None receive benefits in their own right
- ✅ None are in apprenticeships
- ✅ You normally live in the UK (Manchester)
- ✅ All three children live with you
- ✅ No one else is claiming for them
- ✅ The local council isn't paying towards their care

---

### Next Steps to Claim

1. **Apply online or by post** using form CH2
2. **Provide proof of identity** for yourself and each child
3. **Provide birth certificates** for Alex, Blake, and Charlie

### Important Information

- **Payments** are usually made every 4 weeks (or weekly if you're a single parent or receiving certain benefits)
- ⚠️ **High Income Child Benefit Charge**: If you or a partner earns over £60,000, you may have to pay some or all of the benefit back through tax. You can still claim and register for National Insurance credits even if this applies.

### Useful Link
👉 [How to claim Child Benefit](https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim)

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex (age 3) qualifies because: they are under 16 years old, not in paid work, not receiving benefits in their own right, not in an apprenticeship, you live in the UK and are responsible for them (they live with you), no one else is claiming Child Benefit for them, they are living at home in a normal arrangement, and the local council is not paying towards their accommodation or maintenance.

Blake (age 8) qualifies because: they are under 16 years old, not in paid work, not receiving benefits in their own right, not in an apprenticeship, you live in the UK and are responsible for them (they live with you), no one else is claiming Child Benefit for them, they are living at home in a normal arrangement, and the local council is not paying towards their accommodation or maintenance.

Charlie (age 2) qualifies because: they are under 16 years old, not in paid work, not receiving benefits in their own right, not in an apprenticeship, you live in the UK and are responsible for them (they live with you), no one else is claiming Child Benefit for them, they are living at home in a normal arrangement, and the local council is not paying towards their accommodation or maintenance.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You are eligible for Child Benefit for all three of your children. You meet all the requirements: you normally live in the UK (Manchester), all three children are under 16 and live with you, no one else is claiming for them, and there are no special circumstances (hospital, care, fostering, or council payments) that would affect eligibility.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.question: Are you responsible for the child?
response.help_text: Responsibility means living with child OR contributing to their upkeep

criteria: You pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards their care
Contributions include: food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents
Can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments

paths.description: Paying maintenance under court order or agreement
criteria: Payments made towards child's maintenance under court order or agreement
Amount equals or exceeds Child Benefit payment

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to child location checks

response.description: Check if another person is claiming Child Benefit for this child
response.routing_logic: If no other claimant, proceed. If other claimant exists and parties agree who should claim, route accordingly. If dispute exists, defer to HMRC for arbitration.

response.description: Determine if special rules apply based on child's location
response.routing_logic: Check if child is in hospital, care, fostered, or living normally

response.question: Is the local council NOT paying towards the child's accommodation or maintenance?
response.help_text: You generally cannot get Child Benefit if the local council is paying for the child

response.description: Applicant meets all criteria for Child Benefit
response.reason: Meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements

Provide proof of identity for yourself and the child
Provide child's birth certificate or adoption certificate

Payments made every 4 weeks (or weekly if single parent or receiving certain benefits)

Charlie (age 2) qualifies because: they are under 16 years old, not in paid work, not receiving benefits in their own right, not in an apprenticeship, you live in the UK and are responsible for them (they live with you), no one else is claiming Child Benefit for them, they are living at home in a normal arrangement, and the local council is not paying towards their accommodation or maintenance.
